description_long: This house features a complex, L-shaped layout with multiple wings and a mix of stone and brick facades. The roof is predominantly steep and gabled, with several chimneys and dormer windows adding architectural interest. Large, arched windows line one side, while the main entrance is marked by a set of three arches and a stone-clad section. The structure includes both two- and three-story sections, with a prominent central tower and various roof heights. The exterior materials suggest a blend of traditional masonry and red clay roofing tiles. The overall design evokes a historic, manor-like residence with detailed window arrangements and a stately presence.
description: A large, historic-style house with stone and brick walls, steep gabled roofs, and arched windows and entryways. The structure features multiple wings, a central tower, and a mix of two- and three-story sections.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance with three arches, a stone facade, and the central tower rising above the roofline.
description_view_1: This view shows the rear and side of the house, revealing the intersecting wings, multiple chimneys, and a more private courtyard area.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the long brick wing with symmetrical windows and the connection to the main structure, as well as the rear gabled roof.
description_view_3: This view displays the elongated side with large arched windows, stone walls, and the prominent gabled roof, giving a sense of the building's length.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with arched and rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features three stone arches, a recessed doorway, and a prominent stone facade with decorative masonry.
